assessments.status_rationale: Today’s news bullets do not mention CISA, the KEV Catalog, CVE-2026-3888, or Snap-related exploitation activity. No clear evidence that the CVE has been added (or not) to KEV.

While multiple items reference Middle East conflict and energy-market stress, the bullets provide no shipping/logistics data on Ras Laffan loadings/exports or a quantified multi-week drop versus the February 2026 weekly average.
No bullet provides ICE Endex Dutch TTF front-month settlement prices or confirms a settlement at/above €80/MWh on any trading day.
Today’s bullets contain no reference to a Jones Act waiver, its initial 60-day period announced in March 2026, or any extension/re-issuance beyond that.
No BLS PPI release figure is cited in today’s bullets; there is no clear evidence that Final Demand PPI y/y reached or exceeded 4.5% in any monthly release.
Today’s bullets do not report any BLS CPI print or core CPI m/m value; therefore there is no clear confirmation of a ≥0.40% core CPI m/m month.
No item references Micron’s FY2026 revenue guidance or a revision versus prior guidance, so the forecast cannot be graded from today’s bullets.

Although there is defense-related coverage (e.g., Iran war spending discussion), today’s bullets do not mention a specific DoD award/announcement of a ≥$250M frontier AI access/services contract/OTA to Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, or Meta.
No Chrome stable release note/blog reference is included indicating post-quantum/hybrid TLS key exchange enabled by default for ≥50% of stable users.
